    evens = []
    odds = []

    for num in nums1
        if num % 2 == 0
            evens << num
        else
            odds << num
        end
    end

    # Case 1: All original numbers are even. We can choose nums2[i] = nums1[i].
    if odds.empty?
        return true
    end

    # Case 2: There are odd numbers. We must aim for an all-odd result.
    
    # Find the minimum odd number available for subtraction.
    min_odd = odds.min

    # Check if every even number 'e' can be converted to an odd number
    # using subtraction: e - o = odd > 0.
    # This requires e > o, and since we use the smallest available odd (min_odd),
    # we must check e > min_odd.
    for even in evens
        if even <= min_odd
            # This even number cannot be converted to a positive odd number
            # because all odd subtrahends are >= even.
            return false
        end
    end

    # If we pass the loop, all even numbers can be converted to odd,
    # and all original odd numbers remain odd.
    return true
end
